changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to GDPR Article 25, emphasizing data protection by design and by default. Naming conventions are mostly followed, but the audit logging function needed refinement for clarity and consistency with existing patterns.
naming_improvements: Ensure all functions specify the resource they pertain to, such as using 'apiserver' for audit logging
title: Article 25 Data protection by design and by default
step1_reasoning: Article 25 of GDPR emphasizes data protection by design and by default, which can be mapped to several Kubernetes security practices. Ensuring least privilege access aligns with RBAC enforcement. Network policies that default to deny ingress protect data by design. Encryption of secrets at rest ensures data protection by default. Enabling audit logging supports accountability and transparency, key principles of GDPR.
